description: Digital Technologies concept progression map — Knowledge and Understanding strand, covering Digital Systems and Data Representation from Foundation to Y9-10. Concept dependency trees showing how understanding builds across bands.
subject: Digital Technologies

standards.title: Digital systems for a purpose
standards.description: Recognise and explore digital systems (hardware and software) for a purpose
standards.y_goal: Students recognise and explore digital systems, identifying hardware and software components used for a purpose.

standards.title: Data as objects and symbols
standards.description: Represent data as objects, pictures and symbols
standards.y_goal: Students represent data using objects, pictures and symbols.

standards.title: Digital systems and components
standards.description: Identify and explore digital systems and their components for a purpose
standards.y_goal: Students identify and explore digital systems and their components, explaining how they are used for a purpose.

standards.title: Data as pictures, numbers and words
standards.description: Represent data as pictures, symbols, numbers and words
standards.y_goal: Students represent data using pictures, symbols, numbers and words.

standards.title: Digital systems and peripherals
standards.description: Explore and describe a range of digital systems and their peripherals for a variety of purposes
standards.y_goal: Students explore and describe a range of digital systems and peripherals, explaining how they serve different purposes.

standards.title: Transmitting data between systems
standards.description: Explore transmitting different types of data between digital systems
standards.y_goal: Students explore how different types of data are transmitted between digital systems.

standards.title: Data types and representations
standards.description: Recognise different types of data and explore how the same data can be represented differently depending on the purpose
standards.y_goal: Students recognise different types of data and explain how the same data can be represented in different ways depending on purpose.

standards.title: Internal components and function
standards.description: Investigate the main internal components of common digital systems and their function
standards.y_goal: Students investigate and explain the main internal components of digital systems and their functions.

standards.title: Networks and data transmission
standards.description: Examine how digital systems form networks to transmit data
standards.y_goal: Students explain how digital systems form networks to transmit data.

standards.title: Data represented as numbers
standards.description: Explain how digital systems represent all data using numbers
standards.y_goal: Students explain how digital systems use numbers to represent all types of data.

standards.title: Binary representation
standards.description: Explore how data can be represented by off and on states (zeros and ones in binary)
standards.y_goal: Students explain how data is represented using binary (zeros and ones) through off and on states.

standards.title: Hardware specifications and performance
standards.description: Explain how hardware specifications affect performance and select appropriate hardware for particular tasks and workloads
standards.y_goal: Students explain how hardware specifications affect performance and select appropriate hardware for given tasks and workloads.

standards.title: Wired and wireless networks
standards.description: Investigate how data is transmitted and secured in wired and wireless networks including the internet
standards.y_goal: Students explain how data is transmitted and secured across wired and wireless networks including the internet.

standards.title: Representing text, image and audio
standards.description: Investigate how digital systems represent text, image and audio data using integers
standards.y_goal: Students explain how digital systems use integers to represent text, image and audio data.

standards.title: Integer representation in binary
standards.description: Explain how and why digital systems represent integers in binary
standards.y_goal: Students explain how and why digital systems represent integers using binary.

standards.title: Access control in networked systems
standards.description: Investigate how hardware and software manage, control and secure access to data in networked digital systems
standards.y_goal: Students explain how hardware and software manage, control and secure access to data in networked digital systems.

standards.title: Content, structure and presentation
standards.description: Represent documents online as content (text), structure (markup) and presentation (styling) and explain why such representations are important
standards.y_goal: Students represent online documents as content, structure and presentation layers and explain why this separation is important.

standards.title: Data compression
standards.y_goal: Students investigate and explain simple data compression techniques and their purpose.

progression_threads.description: Concept dependency trees for Digital Technologies Knowledge and Understanding, organised by sub-strand. Foundation nodes are roots; children represent the next conceptual step.
